## Harness design corpus (H- namespace)

_Frozen snapshot 2026-07. Project CI never reads the harness repo._

_Collision note: H-0055 = project ADR 0056; H-0056 = project ADR 0057; H-0057 = project ADR 0080. See `docs/adr/README.md`._

| H-ID | Project alias | Title | Status | Decision |
|------|---------------|-------|--------|----------|
| H-0002 | — | Server platform & netcode | Accepted | SpacetimeDB as the authoritative server platform with generated TypeScript client bindings and intent-only reducer calls. |
| H-0003 | — | Shared rule core & client prediction | Accepted | Shared Rust game-core crate holds all game rules; client-wasm runs the same deterministic logic for client-side prediction. |
| H-0004 | — | Client rendering stack | Accepted | PixiJS canvas renderer with a read-only SDK store, pure view functions, and no UI framework. |
| H-0005 | — | Single cohesive repo with a cargo workspace | Accepted | Cargo workspace in one repo holds server-module, game-core, client-wasm, and client to eliminate cross-repo drift. |
| H-0006 | — | Schema evolution & content-sync | Accepted | Additive-only schema migrations; content sync via versioned RON files with build-time validation and a CONTENT_VERSION guard. |
| H-0007 | — | Spatial / zoned subscriptions & per-zone tick | Accepted | Per-zone spatial subscriptions with a bounded intent queue and a scheduled per-zone movement tick reducer. |
| H-0008 | — | Map / content authoring pipeline | Accepted | Zone maps as tiled RON files; monsters, items, and dialogue as glob-loaded content-registry directories. |
| H-0009 | — | CI completeness & merge gates | Accepted | CI gates: lint, typecheck, test, eval, security, mutation, and coverage; every gate has a proof-of-teeth fixture. |
| H-0010 | — | Falsifiable mechanical gates (proof-of-teeth) | Accepted | Every CI gate must have a fixture that fails the gate when the guarded invariant is violated. |
| H-0011 | — | Server-paced movement via a bounded queue + scheduled per-zone tick | Accepted | Server resolves movement by draining a bounded per-zone intent queue in a scheduled reducer tick. |
| H-0012 | — | Client prediction & reconciliation protocol | Accepted | Client applies movement predictions locally and reconciles against server-authoritative state on each tick receipt. |
| H-0013 | — | Netcode smoothness & feel | Accepted | Netcode targets ≤150ms perceived latency with EWMA interpolation, snap-on-teleport, and a sim-harness convergence gate. |
| H-0014 | — | Client app architecture (read-only store, one-way flow, no UI framework) | Accepted | Read-only SDK store feeds pure view functions that produce canvas draw calls; no mutable client state or UI framework. |
| H-0015 | — | Owner-scoped row privacy via RLS (the third visibility mode) | Accepted | Must-never-leak data lives in private tables; owner-scoped rows use RLS; public tables are safe to expose unfiltered. |
| H-0016 | — | Monster individuality & progression model (IV / EV / nature) | Accepted | Monsters have birth IVs, training EVs, and a nature modifier; all three are private and never reach non-owner clients. |
| H-0017 | — | Battle data model & server-resolved turn model (PvP-ready) | Accepted | Server resolves every battle turn with game-core and writes results to battle/battle_state tables; PvP-ready from day one. |
| H-0018 | — | Inventory & item model (one-stack, saturating helpers) | Accepted | One inventory stack per item per player; quantities are capped and quantity helpers saturate rather than overflow. |
| H-0019 | — | Evolution & fusion model (individuality-preserving + content integrity) | Accepted | Evolution is a pure content-driven transform; fusion preserves individuality stats; both are validated at content load time. |
| H-0020 | — | Zone transitions (warps) & subscription switching | Accepted | Zone transitions use a server-authoritative warp table; the client switches subscriptions on warp receipt. |
| H-0021 | — | Dialogue & quest system (data-driven trees + flag-based state) | Accepted | Dialogue trees are data-driven RON; quest state is server-authoritative boolean flags in a private conversation table. |
| H-0022 | — | Currency & shop economy | Accepted | A single private player_wallet table with explicit grant/spend reducers; shop prices and sellback are content-defined. |
| H-0023 | — | Additive battle-depth framework (status / abilities / weather) | Accepted | Status effects, passive abilities, and weather are additive layers wired into resolve_full_turn as discrete pipeline phases. |
| H-0024 | — | Cross-player escrow & dual-consent transactions | Accepted | Cross-player trades use an escrow table with dual-consent reducers; no item moves until both parties commit. |
| H-0025 | — | PvP orchestration (handshake, secret both-submit, liveness) | Accepted | PvP match handshake uses secret-both-submit intent; a scheduled reducer enforces liveness with a timeout forfeit. |
| H-0026 | — | Persistent ranked profile (Elo) | Accepted | Persistent Elo ranked profile stored per-player; rating adjustments happen at match end via a post-match reducer. |
| H-0027 | — | Co-op raid model (additive resolve_coop_turn) | Accepted | Co-op raids use an additive resolve_coop_turn that merges player intents in a single server-resolved turn. |
| H-0028 | — | Social subsystem (chat, guilds, moderation) — untrusted-content posture | Accepted | Chat is untrusted content sanitized at read time; guilds are flat owner-member tables with explicit moderation reducers. |
| H-0029 | — | Observability & performance strategy | Accepted | Observability via SpacetimeDB metrics and client performance marks; latency budgets enforced in CI benchmarks. |
| H-0030 | — | Authentication & account model | Accepted | SpacetimeDB identity is the sole auth primitive; no separate auth service; accounts are identity-keyed tables. |
| H-0031 | — | Privacy & data lifecycle (deletion, export, retention) | Accepted | Player data deleted within 30 days of request; export on request; retention periods defined per-table with a scheduled purge. |
| H-0032 | — | Accessibility strategy | Accepted | Keyboard-navigable UI, contrast ≥4.5:1, and screen-reader ARIA labels on all interactive elements. |
| H-0033 | — | Internationalization (i18n) strategy | Accepted | i18n via a message-key map; locale bundles are data files, not code; the default locale is en-US. |
| H-0034 | — | Security audit & threat model as a launch gate | Accepted | A full security audit gate (M25) signs off the threat model and Semgrep clean before launch. |
| H-0055 | 0056 | Server-module internal module boundary (modularization for fan-out) | Accepted | Server-module source is split into domain submodules registered via mod.rs declarations; cross-module imports are disallowed. |
| H-0056 | 0057 | Content as glob-loaded directories (build.rs) | Accepted | Content registries are directories glob-loaded at compile time by build.rs; the type_chart stays a single-file exception. |
| H-0057 | 0080 | Generated knowledge bundle — OKF-conformant schema projection for agents | Accepted | Server schema is projected as a generated OKF-conformant knowledge bundle; drift-gated by just knowledge-check in CI. |
